_meta.description: Unified schema for all GradMap pipeline stages. Every extracted, cleaned, normalized, and validated CSV must conform to this schema. Do not modify without updating schema_notes.md and bumping version.

year.description: Academic year of the CAP round. Extracted from filename. e.g. 2022ENGG_CAP1 → 2022

round.description: CAP round number. Extracted from filename. e.g. CAP2 → 2

quota_type.description: Quota type. AI = All India. MH = Maharashtra. Extracted from filename. Files with _AI_ are AI quota, others are MH.

choice_code.description: Official CET choice code representing institute + branch combination. NOT the same as institute_code. Preserve raw value exactly as extracted.

institute_code.description: Official CET institute code. Primary key for college identification. Must be present. Do not allow nulls.

college_name.description: Raw college name as extracted from PDF. Will be normalized in the normalize stage. Do not normalize here.

branch_code.description: Branch code as present in source document. Nullable because MH PDFs may not always contain explicit branch codes. AI CSVs typically do.

branch_name.description: Raw branch name as extracted. Will be normalized in the normalize stage using branch_map.json. Do not normalize here.

category.description: Quota-category code. This is the single source of truth for quota, gender, and home university type. Do NOT split this into separate columns at extraction stage. Derive gender and home_university_type in the feature stage from this field using category_map.json.

percentile_cutoff.description: Closing percentile cutoff for this row. Nullable only when truly absent in source. Must be between 0 and 100. Flag rows outside this range as corrupt.

rank_cutoff.description: Closing rank cutoff. Nullable. May not be present in all PDFs. Do not impute or estimate. Leave null if absent.

exam_type.description: Exam source associated with this cutoff row. Extracted directly from AI PDFs/CSVs. Critical for maintaining statistical separation between JEE and CET percentile distributions.

source_file.description: Original source filename. Added at extraction stage. Never modified downstream. Critical for debugging and traceability. If a row looks wrong, this tells you exactly which PDF to inspect.

derive_later_in_features_stage: gender — derive from category using category_map.json
home_university_type — derive from category using category_map.json
normalization_deferred: college_name — normalize in normalize stage using college_map.json
branch_name — normalize in normalize stage using branch_map.json
